messages.content:
The object of a statistical process control (SPC) system is to

provide a signal when assignable causes of variation are present.

assess the customer expectations.

provide a signal when natural causes of variation are present.

eliminate natural causes of variation.
provide a signal when assignable causes of variation are present.

What does the Central Limit Theorem state?

If the sample size increases then the sampling distribution must approach normal distribution

If the sample size decreases then the sample distribution must approach exponential distribution

If the sample size increases then the sampling distribution must approach an exponential distribution

If the sample size decreases then the sample distribution must approach normal distribution

If the sample size increases, then the sampling distribution of the mean of a random sample will approach a normal distribution, regardless of the underlying distribution of the population from which the sample was taken.

Which of the following statement is true?

None of the above responses

A defect item contains many defectives

Defect and Defective are synonyms

A defective item contains many defects
A defective item contains one or more defects.

In transportation problem with 3 supply points and 5 demand points, how many number of constraints are required in its LP formulation?

6

8

15

None of these responses

The number of constraints required in the LP formulation of a transportation problem with 3 supply points and 5 demand points is 8. Specifically, there will be three supply constraints (one for each supply point) and five demand constraints (one for each demand point), for a total of eight constraints.

Consider the following LP problem
Max X + 2Y
Subject to
X – 3Y ≥ 0
X ≤ 3
X, Y ≥ 0
The shape of the feasible region is

a point

a line segment

a triangle

a pentagon

The shape of the feasible region of the given LP problem is a triangle. 

The constraint X ≤ 3 represents a vertical line at X=3 and the constraint X – 3Y ≥ 0 represents a line with a y-intercept of 0 and a slope of X/3. The feasible region is the area enclosed by the vertical line and the line X – 3Y = 0. 

Solving this system of inequalities and graphing their resultant area, we get a triangular feasible region with vertices at (0,0), (3,0), and (9,3).

The first step in formulating a linear programming (LP) problem is:

plot a graph.

perform the sensitivity analysis.

identify and define the decision variables

fin out the redundant constraints.

The first step in formulating a linear programming (LP) problem is to identify and define the decision variables. 

Decision variables are the unknowns for which we need to find optimal values in an LP problem. They represent the quantities that we need to decide on, such as the number of units produced of each product, the quantities to be transported from one location to another, etc. 

Once the decision variables are identified and defined, we can form the objective function and constraints involving these variables.

In graphical method of LP problem if the iso-cost line coincides with a side of region of basic feasible solutions we get:

unique optimum solution.

unbounded optimum solution

Multiple optimum solutions.

No feasible solution

If the iso-cost line coincides with a side of the region of basic feasible solutions, we get multiple optimum solutions. 

This is because when the iso-cost line passes through a corner point of the feasible region, a unique optimal solution is obtained. However, when it passes through an edge of the feasible region, multiple optimal solutions are obtained, all of which have the same objective function value. This is because as we move along the edge, the objective function remains constant. Therefore, any point on that edge is an optimal solution.

feasible solution to a LP problem…

must optimize the value of the objective function.

need not satisfy all of the constraints, only some of them.

must satisfy all the constraints of the problem simultaneously.

must be a corner point of the feasible region.

A feasible solution to a LP problem must satisfy all the constraints of the problem simultaneously. 

In linear programming, a feasible solution is any point within the feasible region of the problem, i.e., the region enclosed by all the constraints of the problem. And to be a feasible solution, it must satisfy all the constraints of the problem simultaneously. The objective function value of a feasible solution need not be optimal.

While solving a LP problem infeasibility may be removed by:

adding another constraint.

adding another variable.

adding another variable.

removing a constraint
أخلِ اختياري

Infeasibility in a LP problem may be removed by either removing a constraint or relaxing a constraint. 

Infeasibility in an LP problem occurs when the constraints are contradictory, i.e., there is no feasible solution that satisfies all the constraints simultaneously. One way to remove infeasibility is to remove a constraint or relax a constraint, which means either removing a constraint altogether or loosening its limits. Another way to remove infeasibility is to add a constraint that will convert the infeasible region into a feasible region.

In assignment problem, if there were n workers and n jobs there would be?

(n-1)! possible solutions.

n! possible solutions.

n2 possible solutions.

n possible solutions.

In an assignment problem, if there are n workers and n jobs, there would be n! possible solutions.

This is because each worker can be assigned to any one of the n jobs, and once a worker is assigned to a job, the number of workers available for the remaining jobs reduces by 1. Therefore, the total number of possible assignments is n × (n-1) × (n-2) × ... × 2 × 1, which is equal to n!.

In operations Research, in which situation, standard mathematical formulation does not exist?

Dynamic Programming.

Goal Programming.

Linear Programming.

Integer Linear Programming.

In operations research, in the situation where the objective and constraints cannot be expressed mathematically in a standard form, Goal Programming is used. 

Linear programming and integer linear programming have standard mathematical formulations to solve for the optimal solution. Similarly, dynamic programming is used for problems where decisions are made in stages and the problem exhibits optimal substructure properties. 

However, Goal programming is a branch of mathematical programming that deals with optimizing multiple and, at times, conflicting goals which cannot be expressed in an exact mathematical equation. It relies on soft constraints and allows for some deviations from the objectives to be accepted.

In operations Research, which of the following statements is correct?

The minimal-spanning tree technique finds the shortest route to a series of destinations.

In the minimal-spanning tree technique, it is necessary to start at the last node in the network.

The shortest-route technique is the same as the minimal-spanning tree technique.

The maximal-flow technique would be helpful to city planners in determining how freeways should be expanded.

The minimal-spanning tree technique finds the shortest route to a series of destinations is incorrect.

The minimal-spanning tree technique is used to find the minimum cost tree connecting all nodes in a network where each node represents a location and each edge represents a path between the locations with associated costs. 

The shortest-route technique is used to determine the shortest path or route between two nodes in a network. This can be used to find the shortest route to a series of destinations by finding the shortest path between each consecutive pair of destinations. 

The maximal-flow technique is used to determine the maximum flow that can be transferred through a network subject to capacity and other constraints. This could be useful to city planners in determining how to allocate traffic flow and determine where to expand road capacity.

In operations Research, the purpose of the MODI method (named also UV method) is to:

develop the initial solution to the transportation problem.

assist one in moving from an initial feasible solution to the optimal solution.

determine whether a given solution is feasible or not.

identify the relevant costs in a transportation problem.

The purpose of the MODI method (named also UV method) in operations research is to assist one in moving from an initial feasible solution to the optimal solution for a transportation problem.

MODI (Modified Distribution) method is an improved version of the stepping stone method used to find the optimal solution of a transportation problem, one of the most common problems in operations research. It helps compute the opportunity cost for each cell of the transportation tableau and provides a framework to determine which cells to trade-off in order to improve the solution. This method assists in making the necessary exchanges in the initial feasible solution until the optimal solution is reached.

A goal programming formulation contains three goals. Suppose that the first goal (G1) have the same importance as the third goal (G3). In addition, G3 is twice as important as the second goal (G2). Which of the following objective function using the weights method is correct?

Maximize (G1 + 2G2 + G3).

Maximize (2G1 + G2 + 2G3).

Minimize (G1 + 2G2 + G3).

Minimize (2G1 + G2 + 2G3).

Using the weights method to formulate the objective function for the given goal programming problem, we need to assign weights to each goal to reflect its relative importance. 

Suppose the weights for G1, G2, and G3 are w1, w2, and w3, respectively. Since G1 and G3 have the same importance, we can assume that w1 = w3 and since G3 is twice as important as G2, we can assume that w3 = 2w2.

Therefore, the correct objective function using the weights method is: 

Maximize (w1G1 + w2G2 + w3G3) 

Substituting w1 = w3 and w3 = 2w2, we get: 

Maximize (w1G1 + w2G2 + 2w2G3) 

Maximize (G1 + 2G2 + 2G3) 

Thus, the correct answer is to maximize (G1 + 2G2 + 2G3).

In transportation problem with N supply points and M demand points, how many number of constraints are required in its Linear Programming formulation?

N – M

N + M – 1

N x M

None of the above responses.

In a transportation problem with N supply points and M demand points, the number of constraints required in the Linear Programming (LP) formulation is N + M - 1. 

There are N supply constraints, one for each supply point, and M demand constraints, one for each demand point. However, since the total supply must equal the total demand, we have one extra constraint. Therefore, the total number of constraints is N + M - 1.

You want an easy-to-use sales forecasting method that can account for seasonality. Which method would be best under these circumstances?

Moving Averages Method.

Delphi Method.

Decomposition Method.

Trend-adjusted Exponential Smoothing Method.

The Decomposition Method would be the best method to use under the given circumstances of wanting an easy-to-use sales forecasting method that can account for seasonality.

The decomposition method is a time series forecasting method that accounts for seasonality by decomposing the time series into its various components, namely trend, seasonality, and noise. It then forecasts each component separately before recombining them to generate the final forecast. This method is relatively simple to use and does not require a lot of technical expertise. It is also able to handle time series with multiple levels of seasonality.

The inventory investment metric that respond to following question: “How many times in a year the inventory was purchased and then sold?” is named:

The inventory turnover.

The inventory weeks of supply.

The inventory days of supply.

The day’s sales in inventory.

The inventory turnover metric responds to the question, "How many times in a year the inventory was purchased and then sold?" 

Inventory turnover is a financial ratio that measures the number of times a company sells and replaces its stock of goods during a period of time. It is calculated by dividing the Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) by the average inventory value over the same period. A higher inventory turnover ratio is generally considered better as it indicates that the company is selling its inventory more frequently, which translates into higher sales and profits.

Which of the following production strategy is generally used in Burgers Restaurant?

Make to Stock.

Made to Order.

Assemble to Order.

Engineer to Order.

Made to Order is generally used as a production strategy in a Burger Restaurant. 

Made to Order is a production strategy in which products are only manufactured or prepared once a confirmed order has been received. In the context of a Burger Restaurant, this means that the burger is prepared only when the customer places the order. This production strategy allows the burger restaurant to accommodate specific customer preferences and provide fresh, made-to-order food items. It also helps to reduce waste and inventory costs, as ingredients are only used to make the exact number of burgers that have been ordered.
